Meta.title: yhilbot — Discord media editor
Meta.description: GIFs, captions, and a real video editor inside Discord. ffmpeg-powered. No watermarks, no upsells.

Hero.title: Cut, caption, render — without leaving the chat.
Hero.subtitle: Five slash commands. ffmpeg under the hood. A real timeline-based editor that fits Discord's 25 MB limit on the first try, not the fifth.

Commands.title: Five commands. Zero clutter.
Commands.subtitle: Each slash command does one thing well. Nothing else.

gif.name: /gif
gif.detail: Auto-tunes resolution, FPS, and quality so the result fits Discord's 25 MB cap on the first attempt — even for 5-minute clips.

caption.name: /caption
caption.detail: Impact font, white background, hard contrast. Up to four parallel jobs so you don't queue behind GIF renders.

edit.name: /edit
edit.summary: QUAD timeline editor: split, text overlays, speed, FPS, undo / redo.
edit.detail: Click-to-edit cursor, in-place modals for precise timestamps, share-code for read-only viewers, persistent sessions across restarts.

stats.name: /stats
stats.detail: Render counts, queue depth, CPU / RAM, disk, log size — everything you need to know if your VPS is on fire.

help.name: /help
help.detail: Reads the same metadata that Discord uses, so it never drifts out of sync with the real implementation.

Features.title: Engineered, not duct-taped.
Features.subtitle: v5.2 fixed seven critical bugs from the v5.1 audit. The list below is the reason.

security.title: No filtergraph injection
security.body: Overlay text is piped through drawtext=textfile= so user input is never parsed as ffmpeg syntax. Color values are validated against a whitelist + hex regex.

concurrency.title: Render concurrency control
concurrency.body: A configurable semaphore caps simultaneous renders. Two users hitting Render at once no longer melts the box.

cache.title: Smart render cache
cache.body: Cache key is derived from the timeline signature without the cursor position, so scrubbing the preview never invalidates a cached render.

sessions.title: Persistent sessions
sessions.body: SQLite WAL with thread-local connections. Restart the bot and your timeline survives — keyed by the real message ID, not a one-shot interaction token.

i18n.title: Three languages
i18n.body: This site speaks English, українську, and русский. The bot itself stays neutral so you don't have to teach Discord how to spell your name.

open.title: Open source, MIT
open.body: Read the code, fork it, send a pull request. No telemetry, no analytics, no hidden API keys.

Status.title: Live status
Status.subtitle: Direct read from the running bot. No proxies, no caches longer than 5 seconds.
Status.demoNotice: Demo data — wire the bot's /api/status endpoint to make this live.

loginRequired.title: Owner area
loginRequired.body: Sign in with Discord to access render history, server-side limits, and audit logs.
loginRequired.note: Sign-in is gated by the OWNER_ID environment variable on the bot side. Only that account is granted dashboard access.

comingSoon.title: Dashboard preview
comingSoon.body: Discord OAuth is scaffolded but not wired in this commit. The page below is what it will show once authentication lands.

Editor.title: Web editor
Editor.subtitle: The /edit command, in your browser. Same ffmpeg pipeline, same 25 MB target.
Editor.explainer: The desktop-class editor is being ported from the Discord-side QUAD UI. This page reserves the route, the layout, and the keyboard shortcuts. The render service runs on the same machine as the bot — not in your browser — so 4K input doesn't melt your laptop.

demo.title: What you'll get
items: Text overlays with safe color picker (no filtergraph injection)
Speed, FPS, resolution presets that survive page reloads
Live size prediction before you hit render
Share-code links — read-only timeline previews for your team

Footer.tagline: Built with ffmpeg, discord.py, and an opinion about defaults.
Footer.rights: MIT licensed. No warranty, no telemetry.
